Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit) under standard atmospheric pressure, which is typically measured as sea level conditions. This boiling point decreases with increasing altitude due to lower atmospheric pressure.

api.text:
# Boiling Point of Water

Water boils at **100°C (212°F)** at standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm or 101.3 kPa) at sea level.

## Important Note:
